calendar.matters:
Order To Show Cause Why The Court Should Not Assume Jurisdiction Over The Petition
Off Calendar Apr-09-2020 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion For Order To Show Cause Re Public Bikes, Incs Petition For Judicial Supervision Of Voluntary Winding Up And Dissolution Of Corporation
Off Calendar Feb-27-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
E. Jeffrey Banchero, Esq. of BANCHERO LAW FIRM LLP, T:415.955.9101, for petitioner/moving party;
Janet M. Brayer, Esq. of the LAW OFFICES OF JANET BRAYER, T:415.445.9555, for creditor/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was discussed and argued. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PETITIONER PUBLIC BIKES INC.'s Motion For Order To Show Cause Re Public Bikes, Inc.'s Petition For Judicial Supervision Of Voluntary Winding Up And Dissolution Of Corporation, while modifying the OSC date as requested by counsel for the Creditor, as follows:

Public Bikes Inc.'s motion is granted. Shareholders and creditors of Public Bikes, Inc. are ordered to appear in Dept. 302 on April 9, 2020 at 9:30 a.m. to show cause, if any, why the Court should not assume jurisdiction over the petition for judicial supervision of the voluntary winding up of Public Bikes, Inc. filed with this Court on November 19, 2019. The court's order together with a copy of the petition and supporting pleadings and declarations shall be served on Public Bikes's shareholders and creditors at least 16 court days prior to the date set for hearing. Public Bikes shall file proofs of service with the court on those creditors and shareholders served pursuant to this order promptly.

Counsel for petitioner to prepare a proposed order that repeats the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, as stated on the record, and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
